task_completion.description: A new collection named 'Warren Court' is created in the Juris-M library.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the left pane of the Juris-M interface, a folder or collection named 'Warren Court' is visible and selected.

task_completion.description: The case 'Brown v. Board of Education' is present in the 'Warren Court' collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: With the 'Warren Court' collection selected, 'Brown v. Board of Education' is listed in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: The case 'Gideon v. Wainwright' is present in the 'Warren Court' collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: With the 'Warren Court' collection selected, 'Gideon v. Wainwright' is listed in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: The case 'New York Times Co. v. Sullivan' is present in the 'Warren Court' collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: With the 'Warren Court' collection selected, 'New York Times Co. v. Sullivan' is listed in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: The case 'Miranda v. Arizona' is present in the 'Warren Court' collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: With the 'Warren Court' collection selected, 'Miranda v. Arizona' is listed in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: The case 'Tinker v. Des Moines Independent Community School District' is present in the 'Warren Court' collection.
task_completion.visual_evidence: With the 'Warren Court' collection selected, 'Tinker v. Des Moines Independent Community School District' is listed in the middle pane.

task_completion.description: The collection contains only the 5 valid cases, excluding cases from other eras and journal articles.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The middle pane shows exactly 5 items. Cases like 'Marbury v. Madison' (1803), 'Obergefell v. Hodges' (2015), or any journal articles are NOT present in the 'Warren Court' collection.

integrity.description: The agent used the Juris-M GUI to create the collection and add items.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the Juris-M application interface being interacted with, rather than terminal commands or scripts.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual Juris-M application.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Juris-M window is visible with its standard layout (left pane for collections, middle for items, right for metadata).

integrity.description: The agent did not simply rename an existing collection or use a text editor to fake the results.
integrity.visual_evidence: The Juris-M interface shows genuine interaction, such as dragging and dropping items or using the 'New Collection' button.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The Warren Court collection must contain exactly 5 U.S. Supreme Court cases decided between 1953 and 1969: Brown v. Board of Education (1954), Gideon v. Wainwright (1963), New York Times Co. v. Sullivan (1964), Miranda v. Arizona (1966), and Tinker v. Des Moines Independent Community School District (1969). Cases outside this era, such as Marbury v. Madison (1803) and Obergefell v. Hodges (2015), must be excluded. Journal articles must also be excluded.
